# Phase 1 Coverage Report — the λ Locking/Conviction Index

**Project:** AFA 2027 QTM Crypto. **Phase:** 1 (λ channels).
**Sample:** 2015-08-31 → 2026-05-31 (130 month-ends); λ computed on `status='observed'`
asset-months only.
**Generated by:** `04_code/phase1_build_identity_map.py`, `phase1_channel1_eth_staking.py`,
`phase1_channel1_evm_locks.py`, `phase1_channel3_voting.py`, `phase1_assemble_lambda.py`.
**Source of truth for *why*:** `04_code/DATA_DECISIONS_LOG.md` Entries 21–29.
**Last updated:** 2026-06-24 (session 010 — Phase 1 close-out: ETH series finished, numbers
reconciled, Channel 2 BTC coin-age re-checked live, N=250 confirmed, gray-zone names revisited).

> **Headline.** λ is built from *canonical* sources per the project directive — on-chain
> event logs (Etherscan V2) for staking/locking and Snapshot's own API for voting, **not**
> from an aggregator. The free-data landscape (no archive state, paywalled staking
> dashboards) means λ is **dense for governance tokens and thin for coins** in this first
> pass: governance tokens get a voting-based λ, 6 vote-escrow tokens get a **two-channel**
> λ (locking + voting), and **ETH (the flagship coin) now has its full staking series,
> 2020-12 → 2026-05 (66 months)** reconstructed from the beacon deposit contract. The λ
> index covers **1,374 observed asset-months across 52 distinct assets** (2020-08 → 2026-05).
> The single largest gap — holding-duration (Channel 2) and non-EVM native-coin staking —
> gates early-sample and coin-side λ, exactly as the spec anticipated. A live re-check this
> session confirmed **no free, panel-usable BTC coin-age source exists** (Channel 2 stays a
> documented gap; see §4.2).

---

## 1. Method (one paragraph)

For each observed asset-month we compute up to three conviction channels, **z-score each
channel within its monthly cross-section** (using only the observed assets that actually
have that channel that month, and only where ≥2 assets share it so a standard deviation
exists), and set **λ = the equal-weighted mean of the available standardized channels**.
Missing channels are **never imputed**; we record `n_channels` and which channels
contributed. Everything joins on `cmc_id`. Output: `03_data/phase1/lambda_panel.csv`.

## 2. Source verification (live, 2026-06-23) — what works, what is paywalled

Per spec §3 step 3, every source was probed before use (full detail: Entry 21).

| Source | Role | Status |
|---|---|---|
| **Snapshot GraphQL** (`hub.snapshot.org`) | Channel 3 voting (canonical off-chain vote store) | ✅ free, keyless |
| **Etherscan V2** (`api.etherscan.io/v2`) | Channel 1 on-chain logs (60+ EVM chains, one key) | ✅ works with free key (V1 dead) |
| **DeFiLlama** (`api.llama.fi`) | registry only (cmc_id → contract/space); **not** a data source | ✅ free, keyless |
| **CoinMarketCap** historical | circulating-supply denominator (Phase 0 backbone) | ✅ (reused) |
| **beaconcha.in** | ETH staking dashboard | ❌ now 401 (paywalled) → replaced by deposit-contract logs |
| **Boardroom / Tally** | voting | ❌ now 401/keyed → Snapshot used instead |
| free public RPCs (cloudflare/ankr/publicnode/llama/1rpc) | historical state | ❌ archive blocked on all |
| Etherscan free `eth_call` at past block | historical balanceOf | ❌ **silently latest-only** → forced event-log reconstruction |

The last two rows are the binding constraint: **no free archive state exists**, so every
historical Channel-1 number is reconstructed from immutable event logs, not state reads.

## 3. Asset → on-chain-identity map (precursor)

`phase1_build_identity_map.py` joins the 1,939 in-universe assets to DeFiLlama's protocol
registry on `cmcId`. Coverage is thin — DeFiLlama's `cmcId` is sparsely populated:
241/1,939 matched, 206 with a token address, **35 with a Snapshot space**. This thinness is
*why* the voting space map is hand-extended and the lock set is curated (below). Enriching
the map from CMC's own `detail.platforms[]` (cleaner per-asset contract data) is the obvious
way to widen Channel 1 later (Entry 22).

## 4. Channel-by-channel coverage

### 4.1 Channel 1 — staking / locking ratio (canonical on-chain)

**(a) ETH native staking** — reconstructed from the Beacon Chain deposit contract's
`DepositEvent` logs (cumulative deposited ETH ÷ circulating), respecting
`staking_start=2020-12-01` (NaN before; PoW). Method **validated** against public levels
(2.17M ETH staked at 2020-12, 5.2M at 2021-05). **The full series is now COMPLETE: 66
month-ends, 2020-12 → 2026-05** (`channel1_eth_staking.csv`), cumulative deposits rising
from 2.17M ETH (ratio 0.019) to 86.16M ETH (ratio 0.714) at 2026-05. **Caveat (unchanged,
by design — Entry 29):** the deposit contract only receives ether, so post-Shapella
(2023-04) the cumulative figure overstates net active stake — it is a **monotone
upper-envelope conviction proxy, not a net-stake figure**. Post-Shapella withdrawal netting
was deliberately NOT added this session (it needs a consensus-layer/beacon source = new
scope, not a finishing touch; Entry 28/29). The ratio exceeding ~0.45 in 2025–26 reflects
this cumulative-deposit overstatement, exactly as flagged in the output `note` column.

**(b) EVM vote-escrow / staking tokens** — locked supply reconstructed from base-token
Transfer logs into/out of the escrow contract, for 6 curated high-confidence escrows.
**All 6 validate to within <3% of the live on-chain balance** (Entry 26):

| token | mechanism | months | first | latest locking ratio | recon vs live |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CRV | veCRV vote-escrow | 69 | 2020-08 | **0.565** | 0.1% |
| CVX | vlCVX vote-lock | 56 | 2021-10 | **0.476** | 0.3% |
| FXS | veFXS vote-escrow | 54 | 2021-12 | **0.179** | 0.4% |
| AAVE | stkAAVE safety-module | 67 | 2020-10 | **0.137** | 2.3% |
| SUSHI | xSUSHI fee-share stake | 69 | 2020-08 | **0.064** | 0.9% |
| YFI | veYFI vote-escrow | 70 | 2020-07 | **0.043** | 0.0% |

Excluded by design (documented, not silently proxied): **veBAL** (locks an 80/20 BPT, not
BAL) and **SNX** (collateral C-ratio system). CVX pre-2022 is understated (locks were in the
V1 locker; the tracked address is CvxLockerV2) — recent values are exact.

**Channel-1 gaps:** non-EVM native PoS coins (Solana, Cosmos, Cardano, Tron, XRPL, …) are
unreachable with an EVM key — the bulk of the 633 coins have **no** staking channel this
pass. Widening needs per-chain explorers or a paid archive tier.

### 4.2 Channel 2 — holding duration / coin-age: **NOT BUILT (documented gap, re-verified)**

A credible coin-age / HODL-wave series needs full per-chain transfer/UTXO history or a paid
fundamentals API (Glassnode/CoinMetrics/Artemis); no free source serves it across the
panel's chains (Entry 24). Per the spec's operating principle we **flag the gap rather than
ship an unsupportable proxy**. The `ch2_holding` column exists in the schema and is always
NaN. Because §3.2 notes this would be the *only* channel for pre-2020 coins, its absence is
exactly what leaves early-sample coins without a λ.

**Free BTC-explorer workaround — checked live 2026-06-24 (Entry 29), before concluding
infeasible.** Per the close-out directive, BTC (the highest-value pre-2020 coin) was probed
specifically against free chain explorers:
- **mempool.space, blockstream.info (Esplora):** expose only per-tx / per-address / per-UTXO
  data, **no aggregate coin-age / HODL / CDD metric**. Computing coin-age from them means
  reconstructing the entire UTXO-set age distribution per month-end — exactly the full-history
  indexing Entry 24 ruled out of scope.
- **blockchair `/stats`:** free/keyless and *does* carry `cdd_24h` (Coin Days Destroyed) and
  `hodling_addresses` — but only as a **current 24h snapshot**; the historical CDD chart data
  is **not** on a free/keyless endpoint (404 / 401 + bot-protection).
- **bitcoin-data.com / bgeometrics (`/v1/cdd`, `/v1/ancient-supply`, dormancy, HODL waves):**
  a genuine free, keyless coin-age API — **the closest thing to usable found.** BUT (i) the
  free tier serves only a **trailing ~4 years** (2022-06 → present; 1,458 daily records), so
  it **cannot reach the pre-2020 early-sample depth that is the whole point**; (ii) it is hard
  rate-limited to **10 requests/hour** (`RATE_LIMIT_HOUR_EXCEEDED`), unusable for a 50+-asset
  panel; (iii) full history sits behind the paid portal — i.e. it is the same
  paid-aggregator category Entry 24 already flagged, with only a shallow recent free slice.

**Conclusion:** nothing free *and* credible *and* panel-usable turned up for BTC, so the
breadth check on other pre-2020 coins (LTC/XRP/DOGE) was not pursued (the depth + rate caps
disqualify the approach before coverage breadth matters; and XRP/DOGE are not UTXO-CDD chains
in the same sense regardless). **Channel 2 stays a documented gap.** No paid source was
procured this session — that decision is explicitly deferred (Entry 28/29). The one partial
positive on record: if a *recent-window-only* BTC coin-age proxy (post-2022) is ever wanted,
bitcoin-data.com's free CDD slice could supply it — but it does not unlock the early-sample
coins that gate H1a.

### 4.3 Channel 3 — voting engagement (canonical, Snapshot)

Token-weighted turnout = mean(`scores_total`) ÷ circulating supply, per month a proposal's
voting **ends**. Space map = DeFiLlama `governanceID` (35) ∪ 27 curated, name-verified major
DAOs keyed by explicit `cmc_id` (56 spaces; Entry 25). A **token-weight validity guard**
nulls 1-person-1-vote/ticket spaces (caught snxgov, enzyme, ilvgov). Result: **55 DAOs with
voting data, 51 with a valid token-weighted turnout, 1,178 valid asset-months, 2020-07 →
2026-06.** On-chain-only DAOs (MKR, LQTY, PENDLE, RUNE, PERP, WLD, ONDO, ENA, Compound
Governor, …) are not on Snapshot → a documented gap requiring on-chain Governor VoteCast
reconstruction later.

## 5. The λ index — coverage

`lambda_panel.csv`: **1,374 asset-months, 52 distinct assets, 2020-08 → 2026-05.** (These are
the final post-ETH-resume figures; they supersede the pre-resume 1,308/1,326 numbers that
appeared inconsistently in earlier drafts of this report — Entry 29 reconciles them. The +66
vs. the 1,308 baseline is exactly ETH's now-complete 66-month staking series, up from the 18
partial months in the first pass.)

**By number of contributing channels:**

| n_channels | asset-months | which |
|---|---|---|
| 2 (staking+voting) | 253 | the 6 vote-escrow tokens: CRV, CVX, FXS (panel symbol FRAX), SUSHI, AAVE, YFI |
| 1 | 1,121 | voting-only 924 · staking/locking-only 197 (incl. **ETH's full 66 months** + 131 ve-token months without a same-month vote) |

**λ asset-months by year × n_channels:**

| year | 1-channel | 2-channel | total |
|---|---|---|---|
| 2020 | 15 | 7 | 22 |
| 2021 | 137 | 31 | 168 |
| 2022 | 229 | 53 | 282 |
| 2023 | 266 | 47 | 313 |
| 2024 | 227 | 53 | 280 |
| 2025 | 188 | 47 | 235 |
| 2026 | 59 | 15 | 74 |

Totals: 1,121 (1-channel) + 253 (2-channel) = **1,374**.

**Distinct assets with any λ, by class:** token 43 · coin 5 · other 4.
- **5 coins:** ETH (66 staking months), GNO (52), SAFE (10), JOE (6), DYDX (1) — ETH via
  Channel 1; the rest via a Snapshot voting space.
- **4 `other`:** SSV (31), RPL (28), MNT (10), OP (7) — all four are gray-zone names (§4 of
  the decisions log, Entry 18) that have Snapshot **voting** but **no security-staking lock**,
  so they earn a voting-λ while correctly staying `other` (see Entry 29 / §7 item 4).

The token dominance is structural: Channel 3 (voting) and the vote-escrow Channel 1 are
governance-token phenomena; coins reach λ only through native staking (ETH, plus the few
mineable/PoS coins that happen to have a Snapshot space). **There is still no λ for any
pre-2020 asset-month and almost none for pure coins** — both follow directly from the
Channel-2 gap and the non-EVM staking gap, neither of which a finishing-touch session could
close.

## 6. What this gates (hypothesis testability)

- **H1b (governance tokens)** — *best supported.* 51 tokens get a voting λ from ~2020-08;
  the 6 vote-escrow tokens get the two-channel (locking + voting-weighted) λ that H1b's
  second sentence specifically calls for. Density is real from 2021 onward.
- **H1a (coins)** — *partially improved.* ETH now has its **full 66-month Channel-1 staking
  series** (2020-12 → 2026-05), so the flagship coin is covered end-to-end; but the 600+
  non-EVM PoS coins and all pre-2020 coin-months still have no channel. H1a remains thin until
  more native-staking sources or Channel 2 (coin-age) are added — and the live re-check this
  session (§4.2) confirmed Channel 2 has no free, panel-usable source, so that gap persists.
- **H2 / H3 (NVT_GL conditioning, quadrant)** — depend on Phase 2 (NVT_GL) and on λ density;
  testable mainly within the governance-token cross-section from ~2021.

## 7. Open questions / recommendations before Phase 2 — **status after session 010 (Entry 29)**

1. **Finish the ETH staking series** — ✅ **DONE.** Full 66-month series, 2020-12 → 2026-05
   (`channel1_eth_staking.csv`), now in λ. Cumulative-deposit method kept as-is per the
   close-out decision (Entry 28/29); post-Shapella withdrawal netting was **not** added
   (needs a consensus-layer source = new scope). Documented as a monotone upper-envelope
   conviction proxy, not a net-stake figure (§4.1a).
2. **Channel 2 (coin-age)** — ✅ **RE-CHECKED, still a documented gap.** A live audit of free
   BTC explorers (§4.2) found mempool.space/blockstream expose no aggregate coin-age;
   blockchair gives only a current 24h CDD snapshot; bitcoin-data.com's free CDD/dormancy API
   is genuine but capped to a trailing ~4 years and 10 requests/hour — it cannot reach the
   pre-2020 depth that is the whole point. No paid source procured (decision deferred,
   Entry 28/29). Channel 2 remains the highest-value missing piece.
3. **Widen Channel 1 cheaply** by enriching the identity map from CMC `detail.platforms[]`
   and adding more curated EVM escrows; add non-EVM staking via per-chain explorers if coins
   matter to the result. *(Deferred — optional, lower-priority item left for a later session;
   not required before Phase 2. Entry 29.)*
4. **Gray-zone classifications** — ✅ **REVISITED (Entry 29).** With real Channel-1 lock data
   in hand, none of the 16 gray-zone names (OP, MNT, MANTA, IMX, RPL, ANKR, SSV, STRD, EWT,
   GBYTE, FCT, BLUR, LOOKS, ME, PNK, PTS) shows a clean security-staking lock: the only locks
   built were the 6 curated escrows (none of these). The 4 with a Snapshot space (OP, MNT,
   RPL, SSV) get a **voting**-λ but stay `other` (governance ≠ security lock); the other 12
   have neither a lock nor voting. **All 16 stay `other`** — evidence unchanged. Per-name
   reasons in Entry 29.
5. **Confirm N=250** — ✅ **CONFIRMED, no change (Entry 29).** λ density is governance-token-
   driven; a tighter (or wider) universe screen would not close the coin-side gap, which is a
   **source problem (no free coin-age / non-EVM staking data), not a universe-size problem.**
   The N=250 rank screen stays as set in Phase 0 (Entries 7, 15).

## 8. Files produced

- `03_data/phase1/asset_onchain_identity.csv` — cmc_id → contract/chain/Snapshot space.
- `03_data/phase1/snapshot_space_map.csv` — 56 cmc_id → Snapshot space (DeFiLlama ∪ curated).
- `03_data/phase1/channel1_eth_staking.csv` — ETH native staking (**complete, 66 months**).
- `03_data/phase1/channel1_evm_locks.csv` — 6 EVM vote-escrow/staking lock ratios (validated).
- `03_data/phase1/channel3_voting.csv` — 55 DAOs' monthly voting turnout.
- `03_data/phase1/lambda_panel.csv` — the λ index (**1,374 asset-months, 52 assets**) + raw channel values.
- `03_data/phase1/_lambda_channel_diagnostics.csv` — per-month standardizability by channel.
- Raw caches under `03_data/raw/` (defillama, phase1_onchain, snapshot) — gitignored, regenerable.
- `04_code/phase1_*.py` (5 scripts); `04_code/DATA_DECISIONS_LOG.md` Entries 21–29.
